title: Delayed Milestones - Cerebral Palsy
chief_complaint: 2-year-old not yet walking independently. Stiff legs, scissoring gait when supported. Born at 30 weeks, had Grade 3 IVH in NICU. Delayed motor milestones.

physical_exam.neuro: Spastic diplegia: increased tone in lower limbs > upper limbs, hyperreflexia, clonus at ankles, scissoring gait, toe walking when supported. Upper limbs normal.
physical_exam.development: Sits independently, pulls to stand but cannot walk alone. Speech: 10 words (mild delay)

hidden_data.mri_brain: Periventricular leukomalacia (PVL) bilateral, consistent with previous IVH

key_learning_points: CP = permanent disorder of movement and posture
Multidisciplinary care: PT, OT, speech, orthopedics

patient_responses.birth: He was born at 30 weeks, had bleeding in the brain
patient_responses.milestones: He sat at 12 months, crawled at 18 months, not walking yet
patient_responses.stiffness: His legs are very stiff, hard to put in a diaper

feedback.correct_message: Well diagnosed! Spastic diplegic CP from PVL.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is cerebral palsy. The prematurity + IVH + MRI findings confirm it.
feedback.attending_note: CP management: Physical therapy (stretching, strengthening, gait training), occupational therapy, orthotics (AFOs). Botox for focal spasticity, oral baclofen or intrathecal baclofen pump for generalized. Regular follow-up with orthopedics for hip surveillance.
